metadata.archetype_justification: Alluxio is primarily a read-heavy data access and caching layer over multiple storage backends (S3, HDFS, GCS, Azure, etc.). Database queries (block lookups, metadata reads) dominate the logic with pagination, filtering, and read-heavy traffic patterns.
metadata.context: Data orchestration / virtual distributed file system. JVM, distributed storage caching layer.

evaluations.title: Asynchronous Operation Support
evaluations.trigger: Service has operations >30s OR long-running workflows

evaluations.title: Infrastructure Capacity for Agent Traffic
evaluations.trigger: Service is P0 priority OR is on the critical path

findings.title: No machine identity authentication suitable for agent principals
findings.description: Alluxio supports SASL-based authentication with SIMPLE (trust-based, no credential verification), CUSTOM (pluggable provider), and NOSASL modes. The S3 REST API uses a PassAllAuthenticator by default that always returns true. There is no OAuth2 client credentials flow, no API key with principal attribution, and no mTLS support.
findings.gap: No machine identity authentication mechanism suitable for agent principals. SIMPLE auth is trust-based with no credential verification. No OAuth2/OIDC support. No API key management with principal attribution. S3 REST auth is disabled by default.
findings.recommendation: Enable S3 REST authentication and implement a CUSTOM AuthenticationProvider that validates agent credentials and maps them to distinct principals. Target: OAuth2 client credentials or API key authentication with per-agent principal attribution.

blocker_remediation.immediate: Enable S3 REST authentication (alluxio.s3.rest.authentication.enabled=true) and implement a CUSTOM AuthenticationProvider that validates agent credentials.
blocker_remediation.target_state: OAuth2 client credentials or API key authentication with per-agent principal attribution, supporting distinct agent identities traceable in audit logs.

findings.title: Coarse-grained permissions — no fine-grained RBAC/ABAC
findings.description: Alluxio uses POSIX-style permissions (owner/group/other with rwx) and extended ACLs. The authorization model is file-system-level, not operation-level. No mechanism to grant an agent identity read access to specific API operations while denying others.
findings.gap: No operation-level permission scoping. An agent with read access to a path can invoke any read operation without differentiation.
findings.recommendation: Deploy an API gateway in front of the Alluxio proxy that enforces operation-level access control per agent identity.

compensating_controls: Restrict agent identity to specific path prefixes using ACLs
Deploy an API gateway with operation-level access control per agent identity

findings.title: No action-level authorization beyond file permission bits
findings.description: Authorization is POSIX permission-based (rwx) at the path level. A user with write permission can create, rename, and delete — no mechanism to allow create but deny delete within the same permission scope.
findings.gap: No action-level authorization. Cannot restrict an agent to read-only within the gRPC or REST API independently of file-system permissions.
findings.recommendation: Implement ABAC or fine-grained RBAC at the proxy/gateway layer to enforce action-level controls.

compensating_controls: Grant only r and x permissions to agent identity paths for read-only scope
Deploy proxy-layer authorization that filters write RPCs from agent requests

findings.title: No JWT/OAuth token exchange or on-behalf-of flows
findings.description: Impersonation support exists via ImpersonationAuthenticator. No JWT/OAuth token exchange, no on-behalf-of flow, and no explicit distinction between agent-as-self vs agent-on-behalf-of-user.
findings.gap: No identity propagation mechanism suitable for agent delegation scenarios.
findings.recommendation: Adapt the impersonation framework to support explicit agent-on-behalf-of-user semantics with audit differentiation.

findings.title: Static credential management with no rotation
findings.description: Credentials are configured via properties files and environment variables. No integration with AWS Secrets Manager or HashiCorp Vault. No credential rotation mechanism.
findings.gap: No secrets management integration. Static credentials require service restart to rotate.
findings.recommendation: Integrate with a secrets management system (Secrets Manager, Vault) for dynamic credential loading and rotation.

findings.title: Audit logging disabled by default and not immutable
findings.description: Comprehensive audit logging framework (AsyncUserAccessAuditLogWriter) exists logging user, IP, command, success/fail, permissions, and execution time. However, audit logging is disabled by default and logs go to local filesystem with no immutability guarantees.
findings.gap: Audit logging disabled by default. When enabled, logs are local files with no tamper-evident or centralized log storage.
findings.recommendation: Enable audit logging for all services and configure log shipping to an immutable, centralized store with retention policies.

ara_metadata.conditional_resolution: Resolved to RISK-SAFETY: agent_scope=read-only, audit logging still important for read-only agents but not a deployment blocker.
ara_metadata.resolution_reasoning: AUTH-Q6 evaluated as RISK-SAFETY because agent_scope is read-only. Audit logging exists but lacks immutability guarantees.
compensating_controls: Enable audit logging and ship logs to an immutable store (CloudWatch Logs, S3 with Object Lock)
Configure log aggregation pipeline (Fluentd/Fluent Bit → S3 with write-once retention)

findings.title: No mechanism to suspend individual agent identities
findings.description: No mechanism exists to immediately suspend or revoke a specific agent identity. The DefaultAuthenticationServer manages channels via ConcurrentHashMap with stale channel cleanup every 3 days. No revocation API or identity lifecycle management.
findings.gap: No API or mechanism to disable a specific agent identity in real-time. No revocation list or token blacklist.
findings.recommendation: Implement an identity revocation API or reduce authentication token TTL to minutes/hours to enable rapid agent isolation.

compensating_controls: Implement short-lived authentication tokens with configurable TTL
Use network-level isolation (security group/firewall rule) to block agent traffic immediately

findings.title: No committed machine-readable API specification for REST surface
findings.description: The gRPC API is defined in 27 Protocol Buffer files (machine-readable). REST proxy API has no OpenAPI/Swagger specification committed. Documentation references MireDot auto-generation at build time but no generated spec in repo.
findings.gap: No OpenAPI specification for the REST proxy API surface.
findings.recommendation: Generate and commit an OpenAPI specification from the JAX-RS annotations in the proxy REST handlers.

compensating_controls: Use protobuf definitions directly for gRPC tool generation
Generate OpenAPI spec from JAX-RS annotations using swagger-core

findings.title: Non-S3 REST API lacks structured error responses
findings.description: The S3 REST API has well-structured XML error responses with S3ErrorCode (25+ codes). The gRPC layer uses AlluxioRuntimeException with RetryInfo. However, the PathsRestServiceHandler lacks structured error responses — exceptions return generic HTTP status codes without machine-readable error bodies.
findings.gap: The /api/v1/paths/ REST endpoints lack structured error responses with error codes and retryability indicators.
findings.recommendation: Add structured error responses (JSON with error code, message, retryable flag) to the /api/v1/paths/ REST endpoints.

compensating_controls: Use the S3-compatible API which has structured errors
Use gRPC API directly which provides RetryInfo metadata

findings.title: No general idempotency framework for write operations
findings.description: Idempotency narrowly implemented only in S3 CompleteMultipartUpload and BlockMaster CommitBlock. No general idempotency key pattern for write endpoints.
findings.gap: No general idempotency key support for write API endpoints.
findings.recommendation: Implement idempotency keys for write API endpoints before expanding agent scope to write-enabled.

ara_metadata.conditional_resolution: Resolved to INFO: agent_scope=read-only, idempotency for write operations is informational only.
ara_metadata.resolution_reasoning: API-Q4 evaluated as INFO because agent_scope is read-only. Read-only agents do not execute write operations.

findings.title: Mixed response formats across API surfaces
findings.description: The gRPC API uses Protocol Buffers (binary). The S3 REST API uses XML. The /api/v1/paths/ REST API returns JSON. Mixed formats across surfaces.
findings.gap: Multiple serialization formats require different parsing approaches for agent tooling.
findings.recommendation: Standardize on the gRPC/protobuf surface for agent integration (most type-safe and tooling-friendly).

findings.title: No rate limit headers in HTTP responses
findings.description: Rate limits are configurable via properties but disabled by default. The proxy does not return rate limit headers (X-RateLimit-Remaining, Retry-After) in HTTP responses.
findings.gap: Agents cannot self-throttle based on response headers.
findings.recommendation: Add rate limit headers to proxy HTTP responses when rate limiting is enabled.

findings.title: No compensation or rollback for multi-step operations
findings.description: No saga pattern, no compensating transactions, no explicit undo endpoints. Journal-based consistency for metadata (Raft consensus) but no application-level compensation for multi-step workflows.
findings.gap: No compensation for multi-step file operations.
findings.recommendation: Implement compensation logic for multi-step file operations before expanding agent scope to write-enabled.

ara_metadata.conditional_resolution: Resolved to INFO: agent_scope=read-only AND data-gateway archetype — read-only agents do not execute write workflows.
ara_metadata.resolution_reasoning: STATE-Q1 evaluated as INFO because agent_scope is read-only and archetype is data-gateway. No multi-step write sequences for read-only agents.

findings.title: No API-level concurrency primitives (ETags, version fields)
findings.description: Internal state lock and Raft consensus for metadata consistency. No optimistic locking, ETag support, or version fields exposed at the API level for client-side concurrency control.
findings.gap: No client-facing concurrency primitives for write operations.
findings.recommendation: Expose version/ETag metadata in API responses for future write-enabled agent support.

findings.title: No circuit breakers for external UFS dependency calls
findings.description: Pluggable retry policies (ExponentialBackoffRetry, CountingRetry, TimeoutRetry) for UFS operations. No explicit circuit breaker patterns. A failing UFS backend causes retries with backoff but does not fail-fast or isolate the failure.
findings.gap: No circuit breaker pattern for external UFS calls. Cascading failures from unresponsive storage backends are not isolated.
findings.recommendation: Implement circuit breakers for UFS backend connections using Resilience4j or equivalent pattern.

compensating_controls: Configure aggressive timeouts on UFS connections to limit impact duration
Master throttle system detects OVERLOADED state and can reduce throughput

findings.title: No per-identity rate limiting at API layer
findings.description: Server-wide rate limiting exists (ThrottleMaster, S3 bandwidth limits) but no per-client or per-identity rate limits. All mechanisms disabled by default. A runaway agent loop would not be individually throttled.
findings.gap: No per-identity or per-client request rate limiting. Rate limits are bandwidth-based or server-health-based, all disabled by default.
findings.recommendation: Enable rate limiting and add per-identity request rate limiting at proxy REST layer and gRPC interceptor level.

compensating_controls: Enable S3 proxy rate limiting (alluxio.proxy.s3.global.read.rate.limit.mb > 0)
Enable master throttle foreground operations and deploy API Gateway with per-identity rate limits

findings.title: No per-identity transaction limits
findings.description: Global operation limits exist (alluxio.master.throttle.filesystem.op.per.sec=2000) but no per-identity limits. No mechanism to configure per-agent transaction limits.
findings.gap: No per-agent transaction limits (max records per run, max operations per session).
findings.recommendation: Implement per-identity operation quotas before expanding agent scope to write-enabled.

findings.title: No draft/pending state concept
findings.description: No draft/pending state concept for file operations. File creation is immediate upon CreateFile + CompleteFile. No approval workflow.
findings.gap: No draft state for agent-proposed changes.
findings.recommendation: Not applicable for current read-only scope.

findings.title: No configurable approval gates
findings.description: No configurable approval gates for any operations. All operations execute immediately upon invocation.
findings.gap: No configurable approval workflows.
findings.recommendation: Not applicable for current read-only scope.

findings.title: No documented staging/sandbox environment with production-equivalent data
findings.description: Docker Compose, minicluster, and Vagrant available for local testing. No explicit staging environment configuration with production-equivalent data shape. No separate staging/production Helm value overlays.
findings.gap: No documented staging/sandbox environment with production-equivalent data shape for agent testing.
findings.recommendation: Create a staging Helm values overlay with synthetic data generation for agent testing.

compensating_controls: Use minicluster for local agent testing
Deploy a separate Helm release with test data as a staging environment

findings.title: Caching layer does not own data classification
findings.description: Alluxio is a caching/data-gateway layer that stores opaque file blocks from underlying storage. It does not classify or tag the sensitivity of data passing through it. No PII detection, no field-level classification, no data tagging mechanism.
findings.gap: No path-based sensitivity metadata to restrict agent access to sensitive paths.
findings.recommendation: Implement path-based sensitivity metadata so agents can be restricted from accessing paths containing sensitive data.

ara_metadata.conditional_resolution: Resolved to INFO: data-gateway archetype caches opaque blocks — data classification is delegated to underlying storage systems.
ara_metadata.resolution_reasoning: DATA-Q1 evaluated as INFO because as a data-gateway/caching layer, Alluxio does not own data semantics. Classification is the responsibility of applications writing to underlying storage.

b1.reasoning: Data-gateway archetype — API responses contain file metadata (paths, sizes, timestamps) not user-record fields. Sensitive data is opaque block content that passes through without parsing.

b2.reasoning: Access differentiation is path-based (POSIX permissions + ACLs). Adequate for a caching layer that does not interpret data content.

b3.reasoning: No formal classification metadata exists. Expected for a caching layer that delegates data semantics to upstream systems.

findings.title: Data residency delegated to underlying storage
findings.description: Data residency is determined by UFS backend configuration (S3 regions, HDFS clusters). Alluxio itself has no data residency controls — it will cache data from any configured mount point on any worker node.
findings.gap: Caching behavior could inadvertently move data across regions if workers are in different regions than the UFS.
findings.recommendation: Configure mount points with awareness of data residency requirements. Restrict agent access to mount points with known residency properties.

ara_metadata.conditional_resolution: Resolved to INFO: agent_scope=read-only AND data-gateway archetype delegates residency to UFS backends.
ara_metadata.resolution_reasoning: DATA-Q2 evaluated as INFO because agent_scope is read-only and as a data-gateway caching layer, residency is determined by the underlying storage systems.

findings.title: No formal system-of-record documentation
findings.description: Alluxio is explicitly NOT the system of record — it is a caching layer. The underlying file systems (S3, HDFS, GCS) are the systems of record. CheckConsistency and NeedsSync RPCs exist for verification but no formal SoR documentation.
findings.gap: No formal system-of-record designation documentation. Agents cannot determine data authority without explicit API calls.
findings.recommendation: Document system-of-record designations and expose cache freshness metadata in API responses.

compensating_controls: Use CheckConsistency and NeedsSync RPCs to verify data freshness
Configure aggressive metadata sync intervals

findings.title: No explicit freshness/staleness signaling in API responses
findings.description: File metadata includes lastModificationTimeMs, lastAccessTimeMs, creationTimeMs. System can signal sync status via NeedsSync. No explicit freshness headers (Cache-Control, X-Data-Age) or consistency_level field in API responses.
findings.gap: Agents cannot determine from a response whether data is fresh from UFS or served from potentially stale cache.
findings.recommendation: Add cache freshness metadata to API responses (e.g., cached_at, source: cache|ufs, sync_status).

compensating_controls: Use metadata sync operations to ensure cache freshness before agent reads
Check lastModificationTimeMs against expected freshness thresholds

findings.title: No PII scrubbing in audit or application logs
findings.description: Audit logs capture user identity (ugi), IP address, command, source/destination paths, and permissions. File paths could contain PII (e.g., /users/john.doe@email.com/documents/). No PII scrubbing or path anonymization applied.
findings.gap: File paths in logs could contain PII. No PII detection or anonymization in logging.
findings.recommendation: Implement path-based PII detection for audit logs if file paths may contain sensitive identifiers.

findings.title: No data quality scores or completeness metrics
findings.description: No data quality scores or completeness metrics. The system tracks metadata consistency (CheckConsistency) and sync status (NeedsSync, GetSyncProgress) but no broader data quality metrics.
findings.gap: Agents cannot assess data quality or completeness programmatically.
findings.recommendation: Consider exposing cache hit rates and consistency metrics as data quality indicators.

findings.title: No API versioning or breaking-change detection in CI
findings.description: Protobuf definitions unversioned — no package versioning, no deprecation annotations, no breaking change detection in CI. REST API uses /api/v1/ but no v2 exists. No consumer-driven contract tests.
findings.gap: No schema versioning, no breaking change detection in CI pipeline. Agent tool bindings could break silently.
findings.recommendation: Add buf breaking to CI pipeline for protobuf schema stability. Implement REST API versioning headers.

compensating_controls: Pin agent tool bindings to specific Alluxio release versions
Add protobuf lint rules (buf) to CI to detect breaking changes

findings.title: Semantically meaningful field names throughout
findings.description: Field names in protobuf definitions are semantically clear and human-readable: lastModificationTimeMs, blockSizeBytes, replicationMax, isCacheable, isMountPoint. No legacy abbreviations.
findings.gap: None — field naming is exemplary.
findings.recommendation: None required.

findings.title: Table/Catalog service provides structured data catalog
findings.description: Alluxio includes a Table/Catalog service integrating with Hive Metastore and AWS Glue. The primary file system API has no data catalog beyond the file/directory namespace and mount table.
findings.gap: No semantic metadata layer for unstructured file data beyond file attributes.
findings.recommendation: Consider exposing mount-level metadata (purpose, data domain, sensitivity) for agent discoverability.

findings.title: No structured logging or trace ID in application logs
findings.description: Full OpenTelemetry integration with auto-instrumentation. Traces exported to Jaeger. However, application logs use SLF4J/Log4j without structured JSON format. No application-level correlation ID propagation.
findings.gap: Logs not structured (JSON). No trace ID in application logs. Tracing is auto-instrumented but logs are not correlated.
findings.recommendation: Configure structured JSON logging with trace ID injection from OpenTelemetry context.

compensating_controls: OpenTelemetry auto-instrumentation provides trace context propagation at the gRPC level
Configure log4j to output JSON format and inject trace IDs via OTEL context

findings.title: No alerting configuration for API error rates or latency
findings.description: Rich metrics published to Prometheus-compatible endpoints. No alerting rules defined — no CloudWatch alarms, no Prometheus alerting rules, no PagerDuty/OpsGenie integration.
findings.gap: No alerting thresholds configured for error rates or latency.
findings.recommendation: Define Prometheus alerting rules for key metrics: MASTER_TOTAL_RPCS, PROXY_*_LATENCY, MASTER_RPC_QUEUE_LENGTH.

compensating_controls: Deploy Prometheus AlertManager with alert rules based on existing metric keys
Configure CloudWatch alarms on proxy latency and error rate metrics

findings.title: Cache effectiveness metrics serve as business outcome metrics
findings.description: Metrics include cache hit rates, operation throughput, latency, and job outcomes. Cache effectiveness IS the business outcome for a caching system. Published via MetricsMaster with multiple sink options.
findings.gap: No agent-specific metrics (per agent identity, per agent path prefix).
findings.recommendation: Consider publishing agent-specific metrics (operations per agent identity, cache hit rate per agent path prefix).

findings.title: No drift detection or comprehensive IaC beyond Helm
findings.description: Helm charts define deployment infrastructure. No IaC for API gateways, IAM, or network config. No drift detection. No explicit peer review requirement documented for Helm value changes.
findings.gap: No comprehensive IaC beyond Helm. No drift detection. Integration surface not defined as code.
findings.recommendation: Implement GitOps-based Helm deployment with PR review gates and drift detection.

compensating_controls: Use GitOps (ArgoCD/Flux) to manage Helm releases with PR-based approval
Implement Helm diff in CI to detect drift

findings.title: No API contract testing in CI pipeline
findings.description: 7 CI workflows with unit/integration tests across multiple parallel groups. No API contract tests, no consumer-driven contract testing (Pact), no protobuf breaking change detection.
findings.gap: No API contract testing in CI. No breaking-change detection for agent-facing APIs.
findings.recommendation: Add protobuf breaking change detection (buf breaking) and REST API contract validation to CI.

compensating_controls: Existing integration tests provide some coverage of API behavior
Add buf breaking check for protobuf schema changes

findings.title: No automated rollback capability
findings.description: Recreate deployment strategy for LogServer. No blue/green, no canary, no CodeDeploy rollback triggers, no feature flags. Helm natively supports rollback but no automation configured.
findings.gap: No explicit rollback configuration. No automated rollback on failure detection.
findings.recommendation: Configure automated rollback triggers (e.g., using Flagger or ArgoCD progressive delivery).

compensating_controls: Use helm rollback manually if a deployment breaks agent-facing APIs
Implement readiness probe failures to prevent bad deployments from receiving traffic

findings.title: No dedicated API contract test suites for proxy endpoints
findings.description: 890 Java test files covering unit and integration tests across 7 parallel matrix groups. No dedicated API contract test suites (Postman/Newman, REST Assured) for proxy endpoints.
findings.gap: Integration tests provide behavioral coverage but do not explicitly validate agent-facing API contract stability.
findings.recommendation: Add dedicated API contract tests for proxy REST endpoints.

findings.title: No service-level encryption at rest for cached data
findings.description: No service-level encryption. Encryption delegated to underlying storage (S3 SSE). Cached data on workers (blocks on local SSD/HDD) and metadata in RocksDB stored unencrypted.
findings.gap: No encryption at rest for cached data on worker nodes.
findings.recommendation: Enable disk-level encryption on all worker storage volumes and configure SSE for all UFS backends.

compensating_controls: Enable Linux disk encryption (dm-crypt/LUKS) on worker storage volumes
Enable S3 SSE for underlying storage and use encrypted EBS volumes for Kubernetes PVCs
